100 for effective collection of outstanding property tax in the area of Shirur Municipal Council, District Pune.

Accordingly, the government has taken the following decision.
The Government's Decisions:
Urban development for effective collection of property tax dues in Shirur Municipal Council area
Department of the. Maharashtra as per the terms and conditions of the Government Decision issued on 19th May, 2025
Under Section-150A (1) of the Municipal Councils, Nagar Panchayats and Industrial Towns Act, 1965:
Government approves implementation of Abhaya Scheme for 100% remission of penalty subject to conditions.
It's coming.
1. Deduction on consolidated property tax due in Shirur Municipal Council area. Completed by the end of May 19, 2025
Pay the property tax due. The amnesty will remain in effect until March 31, 2026.
There will be no rebate on the principal property tax amount under the 2.अभय scheme. It's only
There will be a 100% rebate in property tax penalties.
3. Waiver of penalty on property tax up to 19th May, 2025 by the Principal, Shirur
Ensure 100% collection of remaining tax charges by the Municipal Council.
Collector, Pune.
4. Fourth Annual Assessment of Town Council Households by the Collector to the Chief Officer.
Be sure to start the re-evaluation work immediately and ensure that it is completed within the stipulated time.
Commissioner and Director, Directorate of Municipal Council Administration from the Collector, Pune.
Review the progress in this regard and submit a report on the effectiveness of the scheme to the government.
6.अभय Power to implement the scheme is being delegated to the Chief Commissioner, Shirur Municipal Council.
The property owners concerned should apply to the Municipal Council to take advantage of this scheme.
It's necessary.
7. Any such exemption in respect of property tax may henceforth be granted to the Shirur Municipal Council.
No. Also, any property tax rebate will be admissible to the taxpayers concerned from now on.
No, it should also be made clear while giving this concession.

The city council should do it.

Action should be taken as per the provision of the City Act, 1965.
10. Any reduction in income of Shirur Municipal Council due to implementation of Abhay Yojana.
There will be no compensation from the government.
The Government decision is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
